input.headline: You're a disgrace': Dramatic scene as House avoids Rep. Cory Mills censure vote

raw.headline: You're a disgrace': Dramatic scene as House avoids Rep. Cory Mills censure vote

winner: At the House meeting, even the chairs were blushing when they realized they hadn't been censured yet.

candidates.text: Why was Representative Cory Mills feeling a bit underappreciated at the house party? Because everyone was avoiding him like it was a censure vote.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'avoiding him' and 'censure vote,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity. The humor is mild and could be more surprising."}

candidates.text: Why did the house run away from the censure vote? Because it was terrified of being called a disgrace by Rep. Cory Mills!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat forced and relies on a pun that doesn't fully land. It's brief and specific to the headline but lacks surprise and feels a bit generic in its approach."}

candidates.text: Why did the House decide against censuring Rep. Cory Mills? Because they feared he might write a tell-all book titled "Avoiding Censure: My Scandalous Career in Congress.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the book title reference, but it lacks punch and feels a bit forced. It also doesn't fully align with the headline's dramatic tone."}

candidates.text: A dramatic scene unfolded in the House as Representative Cory Mills successfully led a motion to censure himself for being such a model of decorum and productivity.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with specific reference to 'model of decorum and productivity,' which contrasts humorously with the original dramatic context."}

candidates.text: At the House meeting, even the chairs were blushing when they realized they hadn't been censured yet.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the personification of chairs blushing, but it lacks specificity to the headline and feels a bit forced in its connection to the censure vote."}
